domain: Ethiopian Oromo Gada (indigenous democratic governance; 5 generation classes rotating power every 8 years through 11 grades; consensus-selected 9 Salgan ya'ii Borana leaders; three-principle structure — 8-year terms + balanced opposition between parties + power-sharing higher↔lower levels; 40-year full cycle; genealogical-descent class membership)
specific_mechanism: GADA-ROTATE: rotating-specialization multi-agent governance with 5-class power cycle + 11-grade skill ladder + Salgan-9 consensus delegate + three-principle constraints. (1) 5 agent classes C_1..C_5 each with distinct specialization (factuality, planning, ethics, retrieval, format). (2) Class-rotation cycle: every K turns the primary decision authority rotates through classes (C_1→C_2→...→C_5→C_1). (3) Within-class grade progression: each agent advances through 11 sub-grades on accumulated context-window / authority / reasoning-depth. (4) Salgan-9 consensus: at each rotation transition, 9 delegate agents selected by inter-class consensus to ratify the next class assumption of power. (5) Three-principle constraints: hard K-turn term limit (no class holds power longer), balanced opposition (one class always in opposition role), power-sharing veto (higher-grade agents can veto lower-grade decisions only within bounded scope).
llm_application: GADA-ROTATE multi-agent governance for LLM systems: 5 specialized classes rotate primary authority every K turns; 11-grade within-class skill ladder; consensus-9 Salgan transition ratification; three-principle term-limit + balanced-opposition + power-sharing constraints. Differs from WMAC 2026 AAAI Bridge (high-level program — no rotation cycle). Multi-Agent Orchestration 2026 Codebridge (orchestrated specialized teams — no class-rotation cycle or Salgan consensus). Multi-Agent LLM Preprint 2025-11 (rotating specialization + governance — closest, but no 11-grade ladder or Salgan-9 specific). LLM-MAS Survey 2404.04834 (literature review — covers but no GADA-specific structure). Differs from R463 HOKULEA-CREW (32-house bearing aggregator, not class-rotation), R443 ALUNA-COUNCIL (multi-elder consensus + flag-veto, not 5-class rotating power) by EXPLICIT 5-CLASS K-TURN ROTATING POWER + 11-GRADE LADDER + SALGAN-9 CONSENSUS TRANSITION.
novelty_claim: WMAC 2026 + Multi-Agent Orchestration + Multi-Agent LLM Preprint + LLM-MAS Survey cover rotating-specialization multi-agent LLM governance. GADA-ROTATE adds (a) 5-class K-turn rotating-power cycle + (b) 11-grade within-class skill ladder + (c) Salgan-9 inter-class consensus transition + (d) three-principle constraints (term-limit + opposition + power-share). Recombination — class-rotation + consensus is mainstream.
